notes.title: Untitled Note
notes.content:
Instance-Based Learning

How model learns we classify them.

Instance-Based Learning

Instance-Based Learning

Instance-Based Learning stores training data and makes predictions by comparing new data with stored instances.

How It Works:

  • Stores all training examples

  • When new input comes → finds similar data

  • Predicts output based on similarity

Example:
Predict taxi demand for Zone 4 at 8 AM by comparing with: Past data of Zone 4 at 8 AM on similar days.

Algorithm:

  • K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)
